channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kenya Chosen as Eastern Africa Health Security Hub by Africa CDC
item.description: Kenya has been chosen to host the Eastern Africa Regional Coordinating Centre for health security under a new agreement with the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC). The sig...

item.title: 23,000 Police and CAPF Personnel Secure Chennai for Tamil Nadu Assembly Elections
item.description: Chennai is under robust security arrangements for the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, with over 23,000 police personnel and Central Armed Police Forces (CAPF) deployed across the city. This massive dep...

item.title: Y.S. Sharmila criticises Andhra Pradesh government over fuel shortage, demands urgent measures
item.description: Y.S. Sharmila, the Andhra Pradesh Congress president, has strongly criticised the TDP-led NDA government over the ongoing fuel crisis in the State, calling it a clear case of administrative failure. S...

item.title: SM faces challenges in 2026 but expects continued patronage from Filipinos
item.description: SM Prime Holdings anticipates a challenging year ahead amid economic uncertainties, yet the company remains optimistic about Filipinos’ continued affinity for visiting malls. Despite hurdles such as r...

item.title: Analysis of the Canada Strong Fund and Its Economic Implications
item.description: The Carney government has announced the creation of a sovereign wealth fund called the Canada Strong Fund, intended to invest in strategic Canadian projects and share returns with citizens. The fund i...

item.title: Thunder Bay man receives sentence for stepmother's murder
item.description: A man from Thunder Bay has been sentenced following his conviction for the murder of his stepmother, a case that shocked the local community. The court heard that the victim was subjected to a violent...

item.title: Challenges of Accessing Palliative Care for Children in Rural KwaZulu-Natal
item.description: In rural KwaZulu-Natal, families with terminally ill children face extreme hardships accessing medical care. Thokozile Ndlovu's baby, Xolinathi, suffered from Dextrocardia, a rare heart condition. Her...

item.title: Chinese Trawlers Fined for Violating South African Maritime Regulations
item.description: Last month, four Chinese-flagged fishing trawlers were fined R400,000 by the South African Department of Forestry, Fisheries and the Environment (DFFE) for violating their 'innocent passage' through n...

item.title: Community-led movement aims to end female genital mutilation in Rivers State
item.description: For years, many communities in Nigeria have held on to traditional practices like female genital mutilation (FGM), which is widely recognized as a harmful violation of the rights of women and girls. N...

item.title: Kapamilya Actors Featured in 9 Works Theatrical Production ‘Yemaya’
item.description: ABS-CBN reports that several well-known Kapamilya actors will headline the upcoming stage production 'Yemaya' presented by 9 Works Theatrical. The play promises to showcase a diverse cast and highligh...

item.title: Jay Chou Highlights Chinese Contributions During Australia’s Gold Rush
item.description: Taiwanese pop superstar Jay Chou, widely known as Zhou Jielun, has drawn international attention to Victoria’s Sovereign Hill through his latest music video, 'Gold Rush Town'. The video portrays the e...

item.title: President Ruto Signals Support for Jalang'o in Nairobi Senatorial Race
item.description: President William Ruto has openly shown support for Lang'ata MP Phelix Odiwuor, popularly known as Jalang'o, in his quest for the Nairobi senatorial seat. During a development tour on April 28, 2026, ...

item.title: Teenager charged following house fire resulting in deaths of elderly couple
item.description: A teenage boy has been formally charged with arson and manslaughter following a tragic house fire that resulted in the deaths of an elderly couple. The incident has sent shockwaves through the local c...

item.title: Senator Dela Rosa may choose to decline his salaries and allowances, says Ejercito
item.description: Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa is reportedly considering the possibility of forgoing his official salaries and allowances, according to Senator JV Ejercito. This move is seen as an effort to demonstr...

item.title: Rivers civil servants call on Governor Fubara to attend May Day, promise massive turnout
item.description: The Nigeria Civil Service Union (NCSU), Rivers State chapter, has publicly commended Governor Siminalayi Fubara for his labour‑friendly policies and developmental achievements in the state. In a recen...

item.title: Umar Zahoor files defamation suit in Lahore against Norwegian tabloid Verdens Gang over false reporting on JD Vance meeting
item.description: Dubai-based Pakistani businessman and Ambassador at Large on Economic Diplomacy Umar Farooq Zahoor has filed a criminal complaint under Section 500 of Pakistan Penal Code in a Lahore court against Nor...

item.title: Young Women on TikTok Create Whimsical DIY Cyberdecks as a Rejection of AI and Mass-Produced Tech
item.description: A trending DIY movement on TikTok features young women crafting whimsical cyberdecks—homemade computers often built inside purses or quirky containers. London-based creator Annike Tan, known as Ube Bo...

item.title: Melbourne Football Club CEO Paul Guerra Engages Lawyers After Board Cuts Ties
item.description: Paul Guerra, who was appointed Melbourne Football Club CEO in September last year, has engaged lawyers following his sudden sacking by the club board. Guerra expressed confusion over the decision, hig...

item.title: PS Oluga Urges Robust Political Support to Strengthen Primary Healthcare Across Africa
item.description: During the World Health Summit Regional Meeting 2026 at the UN Office in Nairobi, Principal Secretary for Medical Services, Dr Ouma Oluga, stressed the importance of strong political commitment to boo...

item.title: Karnataka Environment Minister confirms Aero India held on reserved forest land
item.description: On April 28, 2026, Karnataka's Environment Minister, Eshwar Khandre, clarified that the Yelahanka Air Force Station, the site of the biennial Aero India event, is legally classified as forest land. Sp...
